mission.title: Install and Configure Notary Project on Kubernetes
mission.description: Notation is a CLI tool designed to sign and verify artifacts in the OCI registry ecosystem, enhancing security similar to git commit signatures. Installing Notation allows you to ensure the integrity and authenticity of your container images.

steps.title: Add the Notary Project Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Notary Project Helm repository to your Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add notary https://notaryproject.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Notation using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Notation in a new namespace called 'notation' by running:
```bash
helm install notation notary/notation --namespace notation --create-namespace --version v1.3.2
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Notation pod is running successfully by executing:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace notation
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
To set resource limits for the Notation deployment, edit the deployment with:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment notation --namespace notation
```
Then, add the following resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access Notation service
steps.description:
To access the Notation service locally, set up port forwarding with:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/notation --namespace notation 8080:80
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Notation will show the Notation pod in a running state when you check with `kubectl get pods`. You will also be able to access the Notation service locally through port 8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add notary https://notaryproject.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install notation notary/notation --namespace notation --create-namespace --version v1.3.2

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To cleanly remove the Notation release, execute the following command to uninstall it from the 'notation' namespace:
```bash
helm uninstall notation --namespace notation
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
If there are any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) or persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Notation, clean them up using:
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace notation
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Finally, delete the 'notation' namespace and verify that it has been removed:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace notation
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, create a backup of the current Notation release configuration:
```bash
helm get values notation --namespace notation > notation-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade Notation to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade notation notary/notation --namespace notation --version v1.3.3
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Notation pod is running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace notation
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Notation pod is in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace notation
``` 
Look for any configuration issues or missing dependencies.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Notation service, ensure that the port-forwarding is set up correctly:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/notation --namespace notation 8080:80
``` 
Also, verify that the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace notation
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resource limits
troubleshooting.description:
If the Notation pod is failing due to resource limits, check the resource usage:
```bash
kubectl top pod --namespace notation
``` 
Consider increasing the resource limits in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Version mismatch errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter version mismatch errors, verify the installed version of Notation:
```bash
helm list --namespace notation
``` 
Ensure that the version you are trying to upgrade to is compatible with your current setup.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
